stories.headline: US Strikes 90 Targets on Iran's Kharg Island Oil Terminal as War Enters Third Week
stories.summary: The US military struck 90 targets on Kharg Island, Iran's main oil export terminal, escalating the conflict now in its third week. The strikes aim to cripple Iran's oil revenue funding its military operations, prompting President Trump to deploy over 2,000 additional Marines to the Middle East. Energy markets have been rattled as the action risks broader regional instability and disrupted global supply chains.

stories.headline: Trump Urges Allies to Send Warships to Secure Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: President Trump called on US allies to deploy warships to keep the Strait of Hormuz open after Iran warned UAE ports to evacuate, threatening broader Gulf involvement. Iran's threats stem from US strikes on its oil facilities using alleged UAE-based assets. The escalation has heightened tensions as the US sends additional troops to counter Iranian aggression in the region.

stories.headline: War with Iran Rattles Global Economy, Raises Food Crisis Fears
stories.summary: The ongoing US-Iran war is disrupting supply chains and energy markets as strikes on oil terminals have spiked prices and halted shipments through key chokepoints. The economic fallout threatens a global food crisis due to higher transport costs and reduced trade volumes. White House reports highlight these risks as markets react to the prospect of prolonged conflict.

stories.headline: Six US Service Members Killed in Military Aircraft Crash in Iraq
stories.summary: A US refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq during operations against Iran, killing six service members. The crash occurred in the high-risk environment created by ongoing airstrikes and regional threats, following an incident with another plane in friendly airspace. The DoD identified the victims, underscoring the human cost of the escalating conflict.

stories.headline: Trump PAC Under Fire for Using Iran War Imagery in Fundraising
stories.summary: President Trump's political action committee is facing criticism for using footage and rhetoric from the Iran war in fundraising appeals. The tactic capitalizes on national security fears to boost donations amid conflicting messages on war duration. Critics argue it politicizes the conflict and could undermine public support for military efforts.

stories.headline: Synagogue Attack in Michigan Prompts Heightened Security Nationwide
stories.summary: A truck ramming attack targeted Temple Israel synagogue in Michigan during Shabbat services, linked to rising antisemitic incidents fueled by Middle East tensions. The synagogue's prior security investments and staff training were credited with preventing worse outcomes. Similar attacks in Europe have amplified domestic concerns about antisemitic violence.

stories.headline: Microsoft Launches Copilot Cowork to Compete in AI Coworker Category
stories.summary: Microsoft introduced Copilot Cowork, an enterprise AI agent designed to help workers read, analyze, and manipulate files on their computers. The launch follows Anthropic's earlier release of a similar product and reflects intensifying competition among AI vendors to embed assistants deeply into workplace productivity tools. This marks a shift from chatbot-style AI to autonomous desktop agents.

stories.headline: Nvidia Plans Open Source Platform for Enterprise AI Agents
stories.summary: Nvidia is preparing to launch NemoClaw, an open source platform to help enterprise software companies deploy AI agents that perform tasks for their workforces. The platform allows developers to build and run agents regardless of hardware dependencies, reflecting Nvidia's growing push into software ecosystems beyond GPU sales. This could democratize enterprise agent deployment across different infrastructure stacks.

stories.headline: Nvidia Releases Nemotron 3 Super Model for Agentic AI Systems
stories.summary: Nvidia released Nemotron 3 Super, a new open model specifically designed for multi-agent AI systems handling reasoning, coding, and long-context tasks. The hybrid architecture combines Mamba and Transformer techniques with mixture-of-experts routing to improve efficiency and throughput. This positions Nvidia as both a hardware and model provider in the agentic AI stack.

stories.headline: Block Announces 40% Layoff Attributed Directly to AI Capabilities
stories.summary: Fintech company Block (formerly Square) cut nearly 4,000 staff, with CEO Jack Dorsey citing AI as the sole reason for the reductions. This represents one of the most dramatic examples of mass layoffs attributed directly to advancing AI capabilities rather than cost-cutting. The move signals that AI displacement is accelerating in the financial technology sector.

stories.headline: Big Tech Plans Combined $650 Billion in Data Center Spending
stories.summary: Google, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft are planning to spend up to a combined $650 billion on data centers this year, representing roughly 60% increase from last year. The unprecedented spending reflects the enormous computing power and infrastructure requirements driven by AI model training and inference workloads. This capex surge raises questions about returns on AI investment and potential overcapacity.

stories.headline: OpenAI Acquires OpenClaw as Agentic AI Market Heats Up
stories.summary: OpenAI acquired OpenClaw, a viral AI assistant app, following its February breakthrough that sparked rapid ecosystem growth. The acquisition highlights the strategic importance of agentic AI technology and the competitive acquisition activity around autonomous AI agents. It signals that major AI labs are now buying rather than building in the agent infrastructure space.

stories.headline: Ayar Labs Raises $500M Series E at $3.75B Valuation for AI Infrastructure Optics
stories.summary: Ayar Labs, a San Jose-based producer of co-packaged optics for high-bandwidth, low-power AI data transmission, secured $500 million in Series E funding led by Neuberger Berman with investors including Nvidia and Qatar Investment Authority. The massive round underscores surging investor confidence in AI hardware infrastructure amid booming demand for efficient data center technologies. It ties for one of the largest deals in early March.

stories.headline: Vast Secures $500M for Next-Generation Space Stations
stories.summary: Long Beach-based Vast raised $500 million, including $300 million in Series A equity led by Balerion Space Ventures and $200 million in debt, to develop advanced space stations. The funding reflects heightened VC interest in space tech for defense and commercial applications amid a March surge in large rounds. It ties with Ayar Labs as a top deal of the month.

stories.headline: Wayve Lands Record $1.2B for Self-Driving Technology
stories.summary: UK-based autonomous vehicle startup Wayve raised $1.2 billion, marking one of the largest VC rounds in recent memory for self-driving innovation. The funding highlights persistent investor appetite for autonomous tech despite broader market caution, positioning Wayve as a leading challenger to Waymo. It exemplifies March trends toward big bets in transformative sectors.

stories.headline: General Catalyst and Spark Capital Raise Multi-Billion Mega-Funds
stories.summary: General Catalyst is raising a $10 billion fund while Spark Capital targets $3 billion, following Founders Fund's $6 billion close and Andreessen Horowitz's $15 billion. This resurgence of VC mega-funds indicates firms are bulking up for competition in AI, space, and deep tech amid 2026's funding rebound. The trend signals strong LP confidence despite macro uncertainty.

stories.headline: Silverflow Raises $40M Series B in Payments Infrastructure
stories.summary: Silverflow, a cloud-native payments platform, closed a $40 million Series B round in March 2026 from investors betting on fintech modernization. The raise signals renewed VC momentum in payments tech amid accelerating digital transaction growth. It stands out as a key European deal in an otherwise US-heavy funding month.

stories.headline: Plaid Conducts $8B Valuation Employee Liquidity Round
stories.summary: Fintech giant Plaid raised a secondary liquidity round at an $8 billion valuation, down from peaks but still robust, providing employee exits amid tighter market scrutiny. The move highlights shifting VC dynamics toward liquidity events and profitability standards in late-stage fintech. It underscores investors' demand for fundamentals over growth-at-all-costs narratives.

stories.headline: Energy Futures Prices Double in 60 Days Amid Strait of Hormuz Closure
stories.summary: Energy futures prices have more than doubled in just 60 days due to extreme cold weather and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which 20% of global crude oil flows daily. Crude hit $119.48 overnight on March 9, marking the highest weekly percentage gain on record since the 1980s. The rapid price surge threatens global supply chains and consumer energy costs worldwide.

stories.headline: G7 Considers Massive 400 Million Barrel Oil Release from Strategic Reserves
stories.summary: G7 countries are exploring a coordinated release of up to 400 million barrels of oil from reserves to counter skyrocketing prices from the Hormuz closure. Three G7 nations including the US support the move, potentially led by the International Energy Agency, as they hold 1.2 billion barrels collectively. This would be the largest coordinated reserve release in history if executed.

stories.headline: Prolonged Hormuz Closure Risks Shutting In Major Crude and Gas Producers
stories.summary: An extended closure of the Strait of Hormuz could force major crude and gas producers to shut in output within weeks due to storage constraints, fundamentally reshaping global energy markets. Brent crude has surged past $80/bbl with potential to hit $200/bbl by year-end amid extreme volatility. Refiners, traders, and energy-intensive industries face unprecedented supply disruption risks.

stories.headline: EIA Forecasts Brent Above $95/bbl Through May, Solar Rising to 9% of US Electricity
stories.summary: The EIA predicts Brent crude will stay above $95/bbl for the next two months due to Middle East oil production declines, before easing below $80/bbl in Q3 2026. US natural gas prices remain stable at $3.80/MMBtu despite global LNG disruptions, supported by healthy post-winter inventories. On the renewables front, solar is set to rise to 9% of US electricity generation while coal declines 7%.

stories.headline: Global Leaders at 2026 Nuclear Summit Affirm Nuclear's Central Role in Clean Energy
stories.summary: Leaders gathered in Paris for the 2026 Nuclear Energy Summit emphasized nuclear power's essential role in delivering clean, reliable baseload energy amid rising climate goals and grid demands. The event highlighted nuclear as a necessary complement to renewables for energy security during global disruptions like the Hormuz crisis. International consensus on expanding nuclear capacity appears to be solidifying.

stories.headline: IMF Highlights Latin America's Critical Minerals Edge in Fragmented Energy Landscape
stories.summary: Latin America holds strategic advantages in critical minerals including 37% of global copper from Chile, Peru, and Mexico, plus half of lithium resources, amid supply chain chokepoints exposed by Middle East conflicts. The region's 69% renewable electricity mix positions it as a clean energy hub with potential to capture more value. Recent disruptions underscore risks in concentrated mineral markets and create opportunities for diversification.

stories.headline: FDA Approves Leucovorin for Cerebral Folate Deficiency, Walks Back Autism Claims
stories.summary: The FDA approved leucovorin, a generic form of folate, specifically for treating cerebral folate deficiency, a rare genetic disorder limiting vitamin B delivery to the brain. The decision narrows prior broad promises about autism treatment due to insufficient evidence, impacting families who saw prescription surges and pharmacy shortages. It highlights the agency's commitment to data-driven approvals over off-label hype.

stories.headline: MedPAC Recommends $1B Extra for Safety-Net Hospitals in March Report
stories.summary: MedPAC recommended 2027 Medicare payment rates matching current law for hospital services, plus $1 billion in additional funding for safety-net hospitals via a new index to address underpayments. The report covers post-acute care trends, Medicare Advantage, and Part D, urging targeted updates amid financial pressures on hospitals serving vulnerable populations. This will influence congressional budgeting for healthcare sustainability.

stories.headline: FDA Cracks Down on Telehealth Marketing of Compounded GLP-1 Weight-Loss Drugs
stories.summary: The FDA targeted telehealth sites misleadingly marketing compounded GLP-1 drugs like semaglutide as FDA-approved, often obscuring risks and blurring lines between brands and prescribers. Over one-third of reviewed sites implied approval without clear side effect disclosures, prompting enforcement action. The crackdown aims to protect consumers from deceptive practices in the booming digital weight-loss treatment market.

stories.headline: House Republicans Expand Medicaid Fraud Investigation to 10 States
stories.summary: House Energy & Commerce leaders sent letters to governors in 10 states probing alleged Medicaid fraud, demanding responses by March 17 to uncover waste and abuse. The investigation builds on prior scrutiny amid MedPAC discussions on payment access and accountability across state programs. It could lead to tighter oversight and reforms in Medicaid programs serving tens of millions of Americans.

stories.headline: CMS Issues Guidance Strengthening Organ Donation Protections
stories.summary: CMS released guidance clarifying roles of organ procurement organizations and hospitals in transplantation, enhancing patient dignity, trust, and oversight. The guidance aligns with broader priorities around prevention, quality, and data transparency in health systems. It addresses longstanding vulnerabilities in organ procurement to improve safety and equity for donors and recipients.

stories.headline: Utah Measles Outbreak Surpasses 400 Cases as Other States Slow
stories.summary: Utah's measles outbreak, which started last year, has exceeded 400 cases and spread to urban areas, while Arizona and South Carolina report slowdowns after hundreds of infections. The persistence underscores ongoing vaccination gaps and public health challenges in containment. Health officials warn that rapid response is critical to prevent a broader national escalation.

stories.headline: ARPA-H Awards $144 Million to PROSPR Program for Human Aging Studies
stories.summary: The US Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health is funding the PROactive Solutions for Prolonging Resilience program to move aging interventions from animal studies into human trials. The program aims to validate biomarkers that can track aging and predict health outcomes over 20 years. This represents the largest federal investment specifically targeting aging biology and could transform the longevity landscape within five years.

stories.headline: Life Biosciences Secures First FDA Approval for Epigenetic Reprogramming Human Trial
stories.summary: Life Biosciences, cofounded by Harvard geneticist David Sinclair, obtained FDA approval for a Phase 1 trial of partial epigenetic reprogramming to restore vision in glaucoma and optic neuropathy patients. The approach uses three of four Yamanaka factors to partially rejuvenate cells while maintaining their function. This marks a transformational milestone as the first FDA-approved trial testing cellular age reversal in humans.

stories.headline: $22M ARPA-H Contract Tests HIV Drug to Reduce Age-Related Inflammation
stories.summary: A $22 million ARPA-H contract funds a multi-institutional clinical trial testing an HIV antiretroviral drug to reduce chronic DNA-triggered inflammation in 200 adults aged 60-65. The trial targets LINE-1 retrotransposons, which become more active with age and drive inflammatory responses. Led by University of Rochester with Brown and UConn, it aims to help older adults maintain health, mobility, and cognitive function.

stories.headline: Stanford Reveals Architecture of Aging Through Animal Behavior Studies
stories.summary: Stanford researchers studied African killifish to explore connections between behavior and aging, discovering varied aging paths even in genetically similar animals in identical environments. The research provides insights into fundamental mechanisms driving aging and identifies potential intervention points based on behavioral biomarkers. It suggests aging trajectories may be more variable and modifiable than previously thought.

stories.headline: Brown University Leads Five-Year Study on Retrotransposon Silencing for Healthspan
stories.summary: Brown University researchers, as part of ARPA-H's PROSPR program, are investigating whether silencing LINE-1 retrotransposons can reduce age-related inflammation and preserve overall health function. These genomic elements become increasingly active with age, triggering innate immune responses that contribute to chronic disease. The five-year study aims to demonstrate that this molecular mechanism of aging can be safely reversed.

stories.headline: Dr. Eric Verdin Outlines Three-Year Horizon for First Validated Aging Biomarkers
stories.summary: Dr. Eric Verdin discussed breakthroughs in aging research, predicting that within three years researchers will have the first validated intrinsic capacity markers for measuring biological aging. Potential therapies in the pipeline include CD38 inhibitors that have shown lifespan extension in mice and glucosamine compounds affecting metabolism. The convergence of biomarker validation and therapeutic candidates could accelerate clinical translation of longevity interventions.

stories.headline: SpaceX Launches 25 Starlink Satellites from Vandenberg on March 13
stories.summary: SpaceX successfully launched 25 Starlink V2 Mini Optimized satellites into low Earth orbit from Vandenberg Space Force Base, marking the company's 25th Starlink mission this year. The Falcon 9 booster completed its 32nd flight and landed on the drone ship 'Of Course I Still Love You,' demonstrating the maturity of reusable rocket technology. The deployment advances SpaceX's goal of global high-speed internet coverage.

stories.headline: SpaceX Deploys 29 Starlink Satellites from Cape Canaveral on March 14
stories.summary: A Falcon 9 rocket launched 29 Starlink V2 Mini Optimized satellites from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station on March 14, navigating through cloudy conditions. The booster, on its sixth flight, targeted landing on the 'Just Read the Instructions' droneship. This continues SpaceX's intense Florida launch tempo with back-to-back missions from both coasts.

stories.headline: SpaceX Launches EchoStar-25 Direct Television Satellite for Dish Network
stories.summary: SpaceX deployed the EchoStar-25 satellite into geostationary transfer orbit on March 10 from Cape Canaveral, its first GTO mission this year. Built by Lanteris Space for Dish Network subsidiary of EchoStar, it will provide direct broadcast television services. The launch highlights SpaceX's versatility in supporting commercial geostationary payloads beyond its own Starlink constellation.

stories.headline: SpaceX Hits 600th Starlink Satellite Launched in 2026
stories.summary: SpaceX achieved the milestone of 600 Starlink satellites launched in 2026 during an early March predawn liftoff from Cape Canaveral, deploying 29 satellites. The booster completed its 25th flight with the 581st overall booster recovery. The rapid deployment pace strengthens Starlink's network capacity for millions of users worldwide and maintains SpaceX's dominance in launch frequency.

stories.headline: Vast Raises $500M to Develop Next-Generation Commercial Space Stations
stories.summary: Long Beach-based Vast secured $500 million in combined equity and debt to develop commercial space stations, with $300 million in Series A equity led by Balerion Space Ventures. The funding reflects growing commercial demand for orbital infrastructure as the ISS approaches retirement. Vast aims to provide research, manufacturing, and tourism capabilities in low Earth orbit.

stories.headline: Oscars Implement Heightened Security Amid Global Tensions
stories.summary: Security at the Oscars has been tightened beyond previous levels due to rising antisemitic attacks linked to the Iran war and global unrest. No specific intelligence targets the event, but precautions follow synagogue attacks and truck rammings worldwide. Organizers aim to ensure safety as major entertainment events become potential flashpoints in an increasingly polarized global climate.

stories.headline: MLB Finalizes Short-Term TV Rights Deals, Adds NBC and Netflix
stories.summary: Major League Baseball finalized new national media-rights agreements with NBCUniversal and Netflix for the 2026-28 seasons, expanding its broadcast reach beyond ESPN. Netflix will stream the opening-night game (Yankees at Giants), Home Run Derby, and special events for $50 million per year, while NBC returns to broadcast MLB games for the first time in 26 years. The deals reflect baseball's strategy to reach younger digital audiences.

stories.headline: ESPN Gets Exclusive MLB.TV Rights and 30-Game National Package
stories.summary: ESPN's restructured MLB rights deal includes a new 30-game national television package and exclusive control of MLB.TV streaming, making thousands of games available on the ESPN App. The deal also grants ESPN exclusive local in-market streaming rights for six MLB clubs including the Padres, Guardians, and Mariners. This consolidates ESPN's position as the primary digital home for baseball content.

stories.headline: NWSL Announces Expanded Media Partnerships with CBS, ESPN, and Victory+
stories.summary: The National Women's Soccer League announced expanded media partnerships on March 12, extending agreements with CBS Sports and ESPN while establishing a new partnership with Victory+. The deals reflect the growing commercial value of women's professional sports and increasing broadcast demand for NWSL content. Expanded coverage is expected to drive viewership growth ahead of the 2026 season.

stories.headline: Mountain West Announces New Media Deal for 2026-2032 with CBS, FOX, and CW
stories.summary: The Mountain West conference announced a new media rights agreement retaining partnerships with CBS and FOX while adding the CW as a tertiary partner, along with Kiswe for streaming distribution. The deal runs through 2032 and includes Amazon Prime distribution, reflecting the conference's strategy to maximize reach across traditional and digital platforms. College sports media rights continue to command premium valuations.
